rules:
This card can only be attached to Evolution Pokémon. If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of the turn.

This card provides ColorlessColorlessColorless Energy only while it is attached to an Evolution Pokémon.

If this card is attached to anything other than an Evolution Pokémon, discard this card.

Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Supporter card you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent's turn.

The Metal Pokémon this card is attached to takes 70 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Put 3 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.

Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Lightning Pokémon-GX and Lightning Pokémon-EX, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, and if your opponent's Active Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of the Ultra Beast this card is attached to, take 1 more Prize card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Power Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Discovery-GX
attacks.effect: Count your Prize cards and put them into your hand. Then, take that many cards from the top of your deck and put them face down as your Prize cards. If you don't have that many cards in your deck, this attack does nothing. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Vengeance
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile. You can't add more than 180 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Slash Back-GX
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Blow
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Toy Box-GX
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 5 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fairy Song
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Fairy Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Kaleidostorm
attacks.effect: Move any number of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Magical Miracle-GX
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Fairy Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), your opponent shuffles their hand into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Steel Fist
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Metal Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Full Metal Wall-GX
attacks.effect: For the rest of this game, your Metal Pokémon take 30 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), discard all Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Feather Storm
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Unfair-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard 2 cards from it. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Pulse
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Darkness Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Union-GX
attacks.effect: Put 2 in any combination of Darkness Pokémon-GX and Darkness Pokémon-EX from your discard pile onto your Bench. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), attach 2 Energy cards from your discard pile to each Pokémon that you put onto your Bench in this way. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Acme of Heroism-GX
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), and if it would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their next turn, it is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 8 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Poison Absorption
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, heal 100 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nasty Goo Mix-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. If this Pokémon has at least 4 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), put 15 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tingly Return-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. Put this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rocket Splash
attacks.effect: Shuffle any amount of Water Energy from your Pokémon into your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each card you shuffled into your deck in this way.

attacks.name: Giant Geyser-GX
attacks.effect: Attach any number of Water Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flare Strike
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Flare Strike during your next turn.

attacks.name: Double Blaze-GX
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Fire Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 more damage, and this attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shinobi Mastery
attacks.effect: If you played Koga's Trap from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage. If you played Janine from your hand during this turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Ten-Card Return-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 10 cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Jet Punch
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Elegant Sole
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Elegant Sole attack's base damage is 60.

attacks.name: Beast Game-GX
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card. If this Pokémon has at least 7 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), take 3 more Prize cards instead. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hail Prison
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Water Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

attacks.name: Enhanced Horn
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, flip 6 coins instead.

flavorText: Its horn spins like a drill to steadily hollow out rocks—even harder ones. The coloration of the male is more vivid.

attacks.name: Elegant Swim
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Although known for their splendid tail fins, Goldeen apparently compete among themselves to see whose horn is thickest and sharpest.

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.
If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.

attacks.name: Dual Blizzard
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It swims through the ocean at a speed of eight knots, searching for Pokémon that will become its prey. It's especially fond of Wishiwashi.
It has always been supposed that Seel live only in cold seas. Their having shown up in Alola is a mystery.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Three Strikes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads. If all of them are tails, you lose this game.

flavorText: Spacing out is basically all it does. It turns back into Slowpoke if its tail, along with Shellder, breaks off.

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: There are some places where Slowpoke is worshiped because of a long-standing belief that whenever Slowpoke yawns, it rains.

attacks.name: Wicked Tentacles
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of their Pokémon. If you do, put 3 damage counters on the Pokémon you moved the Energy to.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It fires off ultrasonic waves from its red orbs to weaken its prey, and then it wraps them up in its 80 tentacles.

attacks.name: Bubble Jutsu
attacks.effect: If you played Janine from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It drifts through the sea searching for prey. Its poisonous tentacles break off sometimes, but after a while, they grow back.

attacks.name: Swirly Rush
attacks.effect: If Poliwag and Poliwhirl are on your Bench, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It's quite a gifted swimmer, even among Water-type Pokémon, but it normally spends its time on land.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Although it has become capable of living on land, it spends its time in the water, where its prey, fish Pokémon, are plentiful.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Despite the danger, it wants to come up on land. So it does its best to waddle along, but when an enemy finds it, it rushes back to the water.
Despite the danger, it wants to come up on land. So it does its best to waddle along, but when an enemy finds it, it rushes back to the water.

attacks.name: Rocket Splash
attacks.effect: Shuffle any amount of Water Energy from your Pokémon into your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each card you shuffled into your deck in this way.

attacks.name: Giant Geyser-GX
attacks.effect: Attach any number of Water Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: When tapped, this Pokémon will pull in its head, but its tail will still stick out a little bit.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

attacks.name: Blazer
attacks.effect: Turn 1 of your face-down Prize cards face up. If it's a Fire Energy card, this attack does 50 more damage. (That Prize card remains face up for the rest of the game.)

attacks.name: Fireball Circus
attacks.effect: Discard any number of Fire Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head. It's apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.
Salazzle lives deep in caves and forces the Salandit it has attracted with its pheromones to serve it.

attacks.name: Grass Fire
attacks.effect: Discard a Grass Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The males will do whatever the females tell them. They give the females most of their food. Due to malnutrition, the males can't evolve.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although it's rough mannered and egotistical, it finds beating down unworthy opponents boring. It gets motivated for stronger opponents.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It can act spoiled if it grows close to its Trainer. A powerful Pokémon, its sharp claws can leave its Trainer's whole body covered in scratches.

attacks.name: Caturday
attacks.effect: Draw a card. If you do, this Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won't open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: If you try too hard to get close to it, it won't open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.

attacks.name: Flare Starter
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you go second and it's your first turn, instead search for up to 3 Fire Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: High-Heat Blast
attacks.effect: If you have at least 4 Fire Energy in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It lets out billows of steam and disappears into the dense fog. It's said to live in mountains where humans do not tread.

attacks.name: Find Wildfire
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Flare Blitz
attacks.effect: Discard all Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When one is injured in a fierce battle, it hardens into a stone-like form. Then it meditates and sharpens its mind.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Darumaka's droppings are hot, so people used to put them in their clothes to keep themselves warm.

attacks.name: Grand Flame
attacks.effect: Attach 2 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: Legends tell of its fighting alongside a general and conquering a whole country.
While it's quite friendly toward humans once it's grown used to them, in the wild it must be quite fierce to defend its territory from Rockruff.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flare Strike
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Flare Strike during your next turn.

attacks.name: Double Blaze-GX
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Fire Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 more damage, and this attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

flavorText: This Ultra Beast's body, which is as thin as paper, is like a sharpened sword.

attacks.name: Electrical Signal
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If you find its nest, you shouldn't stick your hand inside. You'll get bitten by an irritated Grubbin.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Chlorowhip
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 Grass Energy attached to it, heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

attacks.name: Tangle Drag

flavorText: Many writhing vines cover it, so its true identity remains unknown. The blue vines grow its whole life long.

attacks.name: Reactive Poison
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gastro Acid
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon has no Abilities until the end of your next turn.

flavorText: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

attacks.name: Burning Venom
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

flavorText: The leafy parts act as cutters for slashing foes. It spits a fluid that dissolves everything.
Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

attacks.name: Shinobi Mastery
attacks.effect: If you played Koga's Trap from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage. If you played Janine from your hand during this turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The scales it scatters will paralyze anyone who touches them, making that person unable to stand.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.

flavorText: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.

attacks.name: Giant Bloom
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.
Smells incredibly foul! However, around one out of a thousand people enjoy sniffing its nose-bending stink.
During the day, It stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: During the day, It stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

attacks.name: Triple Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its wings are covered in toxic scales. If it finds bird Pokémon going after Caterpie, Butterfree sprinkles its scales on them to drive them off.
Its shell is filled with a thick liquid. All of the cells throughout its body are being rebuilt in preparation for evolution.
Perhaps because it would like to grow up quickly, it has a voracious appetite, eating a hundred leaves a day.

attacks.name: Jet Punch
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Elegant Sole
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Elegant Sole attack's base damage is 60.

attacks.effect: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card. If this Pokémon has at least 7 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), take 3 more Prize cards instead. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
